preview_text:
The model projects Canada as a strong favorite with an 80% win probability, leaving Bosnia and Herzegovina just 2% odds of victory. This is a steep assessment that positions the match as heavily one-sided from a statistical standpoint.

The expected distribution reflects that imbalance. Canada's 80% win rate dominates the outcome space, while draws occur in the model's view roughly one time in six. Bosnia and Herzegovina's 2% win probability suggests the model sees little realistic path to three points, implying a heavily constrained match in terms of competitive plausibility.

The model and market diverge sharply here. Sportsbook consensus strips Canada's edge to 53%, assigns draws 27%, and gives Bosnia and Herzegovina 21%—a gap of 27 percentage points on the favorite's probability alone. Polymarket prices align tightly with the books. The dashboard does not arbitrate which assessment is correct; the difference reflects material disagreement on the teams' relative strength or match structure.
